name: Moraine Lake Shoreline
blurb: A slow wander along the Rockpile and shoreline, ten peaks stacked above impossibly blue water.

name: Lake Agnes Tea House
blurb: Switchbacks through old spruce forest above Lake Louise, ending at the 1901 teahouse perched beside Lake Agnes.

name: Peyto Lake
blurb: A short, punchy overlook track to the wolf-head lake — the most saturated blue of the whole trip.

name: Mistaya River
blurb: Fast water carving smooth curves through the limestone slot of Mistaya Canyon.

name: Ink Pots
blurb: Up and over the canyon rim to a quiet meadow where six mineral pools bubble in shades of jade.

name: Johnston Canyon
blurb: Catwalks bolted to the canyon wall, close enough to the falls to feel the spray.

name: Quarry Lake
blurb: An easy evening loop in Canmore with Ha Ling and the Three Sisters standing guard.

name: Hamilton Falls
blurb: A forest ramble from Emerald Lake to a tall ribbon of falls tucked into the trees.

name: Natural Bridge
blurb: The Kicking Horse River punching straight through a wall of rock.

name: Wapta Falls
blurb: Out and back through lodgepole forest to Yoho's widest waterfall — 500 feet of thunder.

name: Takakkaw Falls
blurb: A paved wander to the base of one of the tallest waterfalls in Canada, fed by the Daly Glacier.
